title: hedera poetarum var. taurica
intro: Hedera poetarum var. taurica, also known as the Caucasian ivy or Tauric ivy, is a lesser-known variety of the common English ivy (Hedera helix), native to the Caucasus region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Hedera poetarum var. taurica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is sometimes used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
